codeReview.summary: Reviewed the handoff, worker transcript skeleton, commit `6c9cf52c802d607c9a45c633ab458f4f9e5c5b36`, and the touched source/tests. The packet correctly changes `IntegrationCoordinator.updateFulfillment` and its guard script/test assertions to direct dispatch-like fulfillment rejections to the factory-accessible `/api/v1/dispatch/confirm` path. It also adds the missing regression coverage for numeric, blank, and unknown `companyId` queue-production paths, and the handoff records passing compile, gate-fast, targeted JaCoCo, changed-files coverage (100% line / 100% branch), and canonical-path guard verification.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The queueProduction fail-closed company guard was already present before this commit; this packet closes the round-two review concern by proving that behavior with focused regression coverage while aligning the remaining fulfillment canonical-path literal.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `git show 6c9cf52c802d607c9a45c633ab458f4f9e5c5b36` only changes `IntegrationCoordinator.updateFulfillment`, `IntegrationCoordinatorTest`, `OrchestratorControllerIT`, and `scripts/guard_workflow_canonical_paths.sh`, while the parent version of `IntegrationCoordinator.java` already resolves `requireCompany(...)` through `orElseThrow(... "Unknown companyId")`. The new tests add numeric-company success plus blank/unknown-company rejection coverage.

summary: Pass. Commit `6c9cf52c802d607c9a45c633ab458f4f9e5c5b36` cleanly aligns the fulfillment rejection path with the factory dispatch endpoint and supplies the regression coverage needed to satisfy the changed-files CI gate.
